paraphrase.headline: Qatar's Mosque Visit Programme Draws Dozens of Participants
paraphrase.body: Doha: The Mosque Visit Programme in Qatar has successfully attracted dozens of participants, according to officials. The initiative, which aims to introduce visitors to the cultural and religious significance of mosques, continues to see growing interest from both residents and tourists. The programme offers guided tours and educational sessions, highlighting the architectural beauty and spiritual importance of these sites. Organizers expressed satisfaction with the turnout and reaffirmed their commitment to promoting intercultural understanding.

paraphrase.headline: Four teams to make FIFA World Cup debut in Qatar
paraphrase.body: The upcoming FIFA World Cup Qatar 2022 will feature four nations making their first-ever appearance in the tournament, according to a report by The Peninsula Qatar. The teams set for their World Cup debut are Canada, Ghana, Switzerland, and Uruguay. This marks a historic moment for these nations as they prepare to compete on the global stage. The tournament, hosted by Qatar, is scheduled to take place from November 20 to December 18, 2022.
key_facts: Four nations will debut in the FIFA World Cup: Canada, Ghana, Switzerland, and Uruguay.
The tournament is hosted by Qatar and runs from November 20 to December 18, 2022.

paraphrase.headline: Qatar Launches Initiative to Cut Food Waste
paraphrase.body: Qatar is developing a programme aimed at reducing food waste, according to a report by The Peninsula Qatar. The initiative is part of the country's broader efforts to enhance sustainability and resource efficiency. Officials highlighted the importance of addressing food waste to support environmental goals and food security. Further details on the programme's timeline and specific measures are expected to be announced in due course.

paraphrase.headline: Qatar Security Forces Join FIFA World Cup 2026 Security Operations
paraphrase.body: Qatar's security forces are taking part in security operations for the FIFA World Cup 2026, according to a statement from the Ministry of Interior. The participation underscores Qatar's commitment to international cooperation in major sporting events. Details on the specific roles and number of personnel involved were not disclosed.

paraphrase.headline: Qatar's Prime Minister Receives Call from Iranian Foreign Minister
paraphrase.body: Qatar's Prime Minister and Minister of Foreign Affairs, HE Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim Al Thani, received a phone call from the Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran, HE Seyed Abbas Araghchi, according to a statement from the Prime Minister's office. The call discussed bilateral relations and ways to enhance cooperation between the two countries, as well as regional and international developments of mutual interest. The two sides reaffirmed their commitment to strengthening ties and promoting peace and stability in the region.

paraphrase.headline: Qatar University highlights year of responsible AI advances for humanitarian work
paraphrase.body: Qatar University (QU) has presented a summary of its achievements over the past year in the field of responsible artificial intelligence (AI) for humanitarian action. The university showcased its progress in developing AI solutions that adhere to ethical standards and are aimed at addressing humanitarian challenges. According to a statement from QU, the initiatives reflect the institution's commitment to leveraging technology for social good while ensuring responsible deployment. The update was shared during a recent event or report, highlighting key projects and collaborations that have advanced the use of AI in areas such as disaster response, healthcare, and education. Officials at QU stressed the importance of maintaining transparency and accountability in AI applications to build trust and maximize positive impact.
key_facts: Qatar University reported one year of progress in responsible AI for humanitarian action.
The initiatives focus on ethical AI deployment for disaster response, healthcare, and education.
QU emphasized transparency and accountability in AI applications.
